#  Harness the
#  Power of Quantum
#  Computing
Horizon Quantum is building software infrastructure to bridge the gap between today’s quantum hardware and tomorrow’s applications—equipping developers, researchers, and enterprises with the quantum software tools needed to solve real-world problems.
[](https://www.horizonquantum.com/technology/software-infrastructure)
##  Building the software infrastructure to power tomorrow’s computers
Recent hardware advances suggest that the quantum advantage is drawing near—with the potential to transform industries.
But hardware is only half the picture. No computer is effective without software, and classical code doesn’t run on quantum machines.
Only a few hundred specialists worldwide have the skills needed to formulate quantum algorithms. The question facing the industry is no longer whether useful quantum computers will exist—but who will program them when they do.
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aVKWgHNYClf9orP4_side-illustrationx2.jpg?auto=format,compress)
###  What if programming a quantum computer was as simple as writing a Python script?
Our mission is to unlock broad quantum advantage by building software infrastructure that empowers developers to use quantum computing to solve the world’s toughest computational problems. 
[](https://www.horizonquantum.com/company/mission)
![Logo of Triple Alpha ](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYElUd0YXLCxVSCT_logo-triple-alpha-framed.svg)
##  The only choice for serious quantum software development
With proprietary languages and a powerful compiler, Triple Alpha, Horizon’s integrated development environment (IDE), enables developers to create advanced, portable quantum programs.
By leveraging Horizon Quantum's deployment and execution infrastructure, users can run these applications on today’s hardware.
![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYElwN0YXLCxVSCp_icon-code.svg)
Code at multiple levels of abstraction
![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYElwd0YXLCxVSCq_icon-compile.svg)
Compile programs across a range of processors
![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYElw90YXLCxVSCt_icon-quantify.svg)
Quantify resource requirements
![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYElwt0YXLCxVSCr_icon-deploy.svg)
Deploy applications as APIs
Developers can choose the entry point that best fits their needs—whether writing high-level applications, working closer to the assembly-like layer, or programming directly at the hardware level.
##  Designed to overcome the applications bottleneck
Three interrelated challenges are creating an applications bottleneck—one that threatens to constrain the development of scalable software and slow the commercial adoption of quantum computing.
#### 1. Limited talent pool for algorithm design
The ability to harness quantum effects gives quantum computers their power. However, leveraging quantum mechanics is also what makes constructing quantum algorithms so difficult. Formulating algorithms designed to solve real-world problems demands a deep knowledge of quantum physics alongside domain expertise in areas like chemistry or finance. Only a few hundred specialists worldwide have this skillset, meaning that most developers must shoehorn their problems into a narrow range of existing algorithms rather than create new ones tailored to their use case.
#### 2. Primitive quantum programming tools
Most quantum programming languages lack higher-level abstraction. They take a gate-level approach that forces developers to wrestle with the mechanics of circuit construction rather than focus on the computational problem itself. This granular approach makes sophisticated quantum software development impractical because it requires effort similar to developing a processor for each individual problem.
#### 3. Fragmented hardware landscape
The quantum hardware landscape is diverse and highly fragmented. Each competing modality has its own strengths and limitations. Connectivity constraints, instruction sets, and noise vary widely, meaning software written for one device is unlikely to run efficiently—or at all—on another.
![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aVOoGHNYClf9osIC_icon-0.svg)
###  Triple Alpha is designed to overcome the applications bottleneck—empowering developers to harness the power of quantum computing for practical applications.
####  Key advantages
  * Turing-complete languages
  * Quantum subroutines from C
  * Portable code across processors
  * Processor-optimized compilation
  * Comprehensive resource counts
  * Single-step deployment


[](https://www.horizonquantum.com/triple-alpha)[](https://www.horizonquantum.com/product/early-access)
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aX_p0d0YXLCxVPpm_triple-alpha-editor-no-background-3000%C3%9722521.jpg?auto=format,compress)
##  Pioneering software infrastructure for quantum applications
We’re building the development, deployment, and execution infrastructure to power quantum applications.
[ Development Infrastructure ![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aWT7vAIvOtkhBUb6_icon-infrastructure-1.svg) Our development infrastructure lets programmers pick the entry point that best suits their needs, supporting both higher-level abstraction and lower-level control in a single environment. Triple Alpha IDE Programming languages Compiler & optimisation ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/technology/software-infrastructure/development-infrastructure)[ Deployment Infrastructure ![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aWT7wQIvOtkhBUb8_icon-infrastructure-2.svg) Our cloud-first deployment model lets users perform seamless quantum processing within real-world applications—without managing the underlying hardware. API management Job queue & scheduling Real-time execution ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/technology/software-infrastructure/deployment-infrastructure)[ Execution Infrastructure ![""](https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aWT7xwIvOtkhBUcA_icon-infrastructure-3.svg) Our execution infrastructure empowers developers to run complex, adaptive quantum programs, even on devices that lack native support for general control flow. Quantum hardware Runtime environment* *capability in development ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/technology/software-infrastructure/execution-infrastructure)
[](https://www.horizonquantum.com/technology/software-infrastructure)

Explore quantum programming with our technology. Register your interest for early access to Triple Alpha. Check out our resources to stay up to date on our latest news, events, and collaboration opportunities.

industries.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><p><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->But hardware is only half the picture. No computer is effective without software, and classical code doesn’t run on quantum machines.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><p><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->Only a few hundred specialists worldwide have the skills needed to formulate quantum algorithms. the assembly-like layer, or programming directly at the hardware level.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><!--]--><!--]--></div></div></div></div></div><section class="interactivestepsdefault" data-slice-type="interactive_steps" data-slice-variation="default" data-v-0a481c8e=""><div class="interactivestepsdefaultContainer commonContainer" data-v-0a481c8e=""><div class="interactivestepsdefaultHeader" data-v-0a481c8e=""><div class="prismicrichtext interactivestepsdefaultHeader__title" data-v-0a481c8e=""><!--[--><!--[--><h2><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->Designed to overcome the applications bottleneck<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></h2><!--]--><!--]--></div><div class="prismicrichtext interactivestepsdefaultHeader__text" data-v-0a481c8e=""><!--[--><!--[--><p><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->Three interrelated challenges are creating an applications bottleneck—one that threatens to constrain the development of scalable software and slow the commercial adoption of quantum computing.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><!--]--><!--]--></div></div><div class="interactivestepsdefaultContent" data-v-0a481c8e=""><div class="flex-pseudo-medium" data-v-0a481c8e=""></div><div class="interactivestepsdefaultSteps interactivestepsdefaultItem" data-v-0a481c8e=""><div class="accordion" data-v-0a481c8e="" data-v-ba963dc4=""><!--[--><div class="accordionItem" data-v-ba963dc4=""><button class="accordionItemHeader" aria-expanded="false" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemTitles" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemTitle" data-v-ba963dc4=""><h4 data-v-ba963dc4="">1. Limited talent pool for algorithm design</h4></div><!----></div><div class="accordionItemIcon" data-v-ba963dc4=""><svg class="accordionItemIconSvg" width="16" height="13" viewBox="0 0 16 13" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" data-v-ba963dc4=""><path d="M10 0.353516L14.2929 4.64641C14.7456 5.09916 15 5.71323 15 6.35352M10 12.3535L14.2929 8.06062C14.7456 7.60787 15 6.9938 15 6.35352M0 6.35352L15 6.35352" stroke="#001C3F" data-v-ba963dc4=""></path></svg></div></button><div class="accordionItemContent" style="height:0px;" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemContentInner" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="prismicrichtext" data-v-ba963dc4=""><!--[--><!--[--><p><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->The ability to harness quantum effects gives quantum computers their power. However, leveraging quantum mechanics is also what makes constructing quantum algorithms so difficult. Formulating algorithms designed to solve real-world problems demands a deep knowledge of quantum physics alongside domain expertise in areas like chemistry or finance. Only a few hundred specialists worldwide have this skillset, meaning that most developers must shoehorn their problems into a narrow range of existing algorithms rather than create new ones tailored to their use case.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><!--]--><!--]--></div><!----></div></div></div><div class="accordionItem" data-v-ba963dc4=""><button class="accordionItemHeader" aria-expanded="false" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemTitles" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemTitle" data-v-ba963dc4=""><h4 data-v-ba963dc4="">2. Primitive quantum programming tools</h4></div><!----></div><div class="accordionItemIcon" data-v-ba963dc4=""><svg class="accordionItemIconSvg" width="16" height="13" viewBox="0 0 16 13" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" data-v-ba963dc4=""><path d="M10 0.353516L14.2929 4.64641C14.7456 5.09916 15 5.71323 15 6.35352M10 12.3535L14.2929 8.06062C14.7456 7.60787 15 6.9938 15 6.35352M0 6.35352L15 6.35352" stroke="#001C3F" data-v-ba963dc4=""></path></svg></div></button><div class="accordionItemContent" style="height:0px;" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemContentInner" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="prismicrichtext" data-v-ba963dc4=""><!--[--><!--[--><p><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->Most quantum programming languages lack higher-level abstraction. They take a gate-level approach that forces developers to wrestle with the mechanics of circuit construction rather than focus on the computational problem itself. This granular approach makes sophisticated quantum software development impractical because it requires effort similar to developing a processor for each individual problem.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><!--]--><!--]--></div><!----></div></div></div><div class="accordionItem" data-v-ba963dc4=""><button class="accordionItemHeader" aria-expanded="false" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemTitles" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemTitle" data-v-ba963dc4=""><h4 data-v-ba963dc4="">3. Fragmented hardware landscape</h4></div><!----></div><div class="accordionItemIcon" data-v-ba963dc4=""><svg class="accordionItemIconSvg" width="16" height="13" viewBox="0 0 16 13" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" data-v-ba963dc4=""><path d="M10 0.353516L14.2929 4.64641C14.7456 5.09916 15 5.71323 15 6.35352M10 12.3535L14.2929 8.06062C14.7456 7.60787 15 6.9938 15 6.35352M0 6.35352L15 6.35352" stroke="#001C3F" data-v-ba963dc4=""></path></svg></div></button><div class="accordionItemContent" style="height:0px;" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="accordionItemContentInner" data-v-ba963dc4=""><div class="prismicrichtext" data-v-ba963dc4=""><!--[--><!--[--><p><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!--[--><!---->The quantum hardware landscape is diverse and highly fragmented. Each competing modality has its own strengths and limitations. Connectivity constraints, instruction sets, and noise vary widely, meaning software written for one device is unlikely to run efficiently—or at all—on another.<!--]--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></p><!--]--><!--]--></div><!----></div></div></div><!--]--></div></div><div class="flex-pseudo-small flex-pseudo-line" data-v-0a481c8e=""><div to overcome the applications bottleneck",[],[1154],{"type":1006,"text":1155,"spans":1156,"direction":997},"Three interrelated challenges are creating an applications bottleneck—one that threatens to constrain the development of scalable software and slow the commercial adoption of quantum computing.",[],[1158,1181,1198],{"title":1159,"text":1164,"icon":1168,"content":1174},[1160],{"type":1161,"text":1162,"spans":1163,"direction":997},"heading4","1. Limited talent pool for algorithm design",[],[1165],{"type":1006,"text":1166,"spans":1167,"direction":997},"The ability to harness quantum effects gives quantum computers their power. However, leveraging quantum mechanics is also what makes constructing quantum algorithms so difficult. Formulating algorithms designed to solve real-world problems demands a deep knowledge of quantum physics alongside domain expertise in areas like chemistry or finance. Only a few hundred specialists worldwide have this skillset, meaning that most developers must shoehorn their problems into a narrow range of existing algorithms rather than create new ones tailored to their use case.",[],{"dimensions":1169,"alt":1048,"copyright":7,"url":1171,"id":1172,"edit":1173},{"width":1170,"height":1170},48,"https://horizon-v2.cdn.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aVOoGHNYClf9osIC_icon-0.svg","aVOoGHNYClf9osIC",{"x":777,"y":777,"zoom":60,"background":778},[1175,1178],{"type":1006,"text":1176,"spans":1177,"direction":997},"",[],{"type":1054,"text":1179,"spans":1180,"direction":997},"Triple Alpha is designed to overcome the applications bottleneck—empowering developers to harness the power of quantum computing for practical applications.",[],{"title":1182,"text":1186,"icon":1190,"content":1193},[1183],{"type":1161,"text":1184,"spans":1185,"direction":997},"2. Primitive quantum programming tools",[],[1187],{"type":1006,"text":1188,"spans":1189,"direction":997},"Most quantum programming languages lack higher-level abstraction. They take a gate-level approach that forces developers to wrestle with the mechanics of circuit construction rather than focus on the computational problem itself. This granular approach makes sophisticated quantum software development impractical because it requires effort similar to developing a processor for each individual problem.",[],{"dimensions":1191,"alt":1048,"copyright":7,"url":1171,"id":1172,"edit":1192},{"width":1170,"height":1170},{"x":777,"y":777,"zoom":60,"background":778},[1194,1196],{"type":1006,"text":1176,"spans":1195,"direction":997},[],{"type":1054,"text":1179,"spans":1197,"direction":997},[],{"title":1199,"text":1203,"icon":1207,"content":1210},[1200],{"type":1161,"text":1201,"spans":1202,"direction":997},"3. Fragmented hardware landscape",[],[1204],{"type":1006,"text":1205,"spans":1206,"direction":997},"The quantum hardware landscape is diverse and highly fragmented. Each competing modality has its own strengths and limitations. Connectivity constraints, instruction sets, and noise vary widely, meaning software written for one device is unlikely to run efficiently—or at all—on
